mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and robust economic activity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the strategy produces negative Sharpe ratio out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher equity prices.
failure_condition: In at least 60% of trigger instances the 60-day forward return is negative, or the hit rate is not significantly better than 50% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales are elevated and the federal funds rate is near zero, consumer demand is strong and monetary policy remains accommodative, jointly pulling forward consumption and investment and raising equity valuations.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the median 20-day forward return is less than 0%, or the rank IC of the interaction term is non-positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During labor market recovery phases when the unemployment rate is still high but falling swiftly, strong payroll gains are especially effective in boosting equity markets as they confirm the recovery trajectory.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE > 5.0, the correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP readings reflect the broadest measure of economic activity; above-trend growth in GDP signals a robust economy that supports corporate profits and equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return after a GDP release shows no significant positive mean difference compared to all other 60-day windows (t-test p > 0.05), or the strategy underperforms a buy-and-hold by more than -5% annualized over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating month-over-month increase in consumer prices raises expectations of monetary tightening, which pressures equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions.
failure_condition: After trigger events, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the rank IC is below -0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in payroll employment and industrial production indicate synchronized strength in both labor and output markets, reinforcing confidence in the economic expansion and driving equities higher.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the 20-day forward return distribution is not significantly shifted above zero (one-sample t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term in a regression is not positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer price inflation triggers immediate market repricing of rate expectations, causing short-term negative equity returns as investors adjust to tighter monetary policy outlook.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following triggers is not negative, or the one-sided t-test of negative mean returns fails at the 5% significance level over the full sample.
